play.title: Don't build module-specific error handling/logging frameworks
play.context: Using the platform's shared observability primitives (Rails logger, StatsD, Datadog tracing, and ExceptionHandling) gives the API consistent log formats, span tagging, and error classification across every module. SREs can write one dashboard query that covers all services, and platform-level fixes apply everywhere at once. Custom per-module wrappers fragment observability, require duplicate maintenance, and force developers to learn a different framework in every module.

rules.rule: Use the centralized ExceptionHandling concern and standard APIs (Datadog::Tracing, Rails.logger, StatsD) directly; do not create module-specific wrappers for error handling, logging, monitoring, or tracing.
Propose requirements for missing centralized capabilities to the platform team rather than building module-specific solutions.

investigate_before_answering.step: Read the custom framework class fully to understand what it wraps (Datadog tracing, Rails.logger, StatsD, or error handling).
Identify whether the custom framework contains infrastructure bugs (span leaks from missing re-raise, incorrect span.set_error for all errors, resource leaks from unclosed spans).
Determine which standard API the custom framework wraps and whether the module can use the standard API directly.
Check if the custom framework is a Faraday middleware for HTTP client logging (NOT an anti-pattern) or a domain-specific exception class (NOT an anti-pattern) before flagging.

Verify whether the module's controllers use ExceptionHandling concern or have custom rescue_from handlers that bypass it. Do not flag Faraday middleware, domain exception classes, or module-specific business logic as anti-patterns. Only flag code that creates a new logging/tracing/monitoring/error-handling API that duplicates centralized infrastructure.

severity_assessment.critical: new custom LogService, Monitor, or ErrorHandler class created in a module
custom framework contains span leak (rescue without re-raise inside tracing block)
custom rescue_from handler in module bypasses ExceptionHandling concern
severity_assessment.high: custom framework calls span.set_error for all errors regardless of status code
new module wraps Datadog::Tracing, Rails.logger, or StatsD in custom class
severity_assessment.medium: existing monitor class extended with new methods instead of proposing centralized requirements
severity_assessment.false_positive: Domain exception hierarchies that define typed exceptions for a module's specific error conditions (e.g., `ClaimsApi::UnprocessableEntityError < StandardError`); these are domain modeling, not framework building. Faraday middleware classes that configure connection behavior (timeouts, headers, auth); these are client configuration, not monitoring wrappers. Thin convenience wrappers that delegate directly to a platform method with no added logic (e.g., a one-line `def log_error(e)` that calls `Rails.logger.error`).

play.pr_comment_template:
**Don't build module-specific frameworks** | `CRITICAL`
`{{file_path}}:{{line_number}}` -- {{one_line_violation_description}}
**Why this matters:** 24 modules have already built competing logging/tracing/error-handling frameworks. Each has a different API, each contains potential infrastructure bugs (span leaks, incorrect error tagging), and bug fixes must be applied 24 times. The LogService in ask_va_api has a span leak because custom frameworks miss subtle infrastructure requirements.
**The Rule:** Do NOT create module-specific error handlers, logging services, monitoring classes, or tracing wrappers. Use standard APIs directly or propose requirements for the centralized framework.
**Suggested fix:** ```ruby Datadog::Tracing.trace('my_module.operation') do |span| span.set_tag('module', 'my_module') result = do_work Rails.logger.info('my_module.operation completed') result end ```
**Verify:** - [ ] No new custom logging/tracing/monitoring class in module - [ ] Controllers use ExceptionHandling concern (no custom rescue_from) - [ ] Standard APIs used directly (Datadog::Tracing, Rails.logger, StatsD) - [ ] Exceptions bubble up to ExceptionHandling - [ ] No manual span.set_error in module code
[Play: Don't Build Module-Specific Frameworks](10-dont-build-module-specific-frameworks.xml)

example.name: ask_va_api LogService with span leak
example.bad: class LogService def call(action, tags: {}, &block) span = nil trace_and_annotate_action(action, tags) do |s| @span = span = s @elapsed_time = Benchmark.realtime { @result = block.call } end log_timing_metric(action) result rescue => e handle_logging_error(action, e, span) # Bug: Never re-raises exception # Missing: raise: span leak! end end
example.good: # Use standard APIs directly: no wrapper needed Datadog::Tracing.trace('my_module.operation') do |span| span.set_tag('module', 'my_module') result = do_work end # Datadog handles span lifecycle automatically

example.name: Module-specific rescue_from bypasses ExceptionHandling
example.bad:
class MyModuleController < ApplicationController rescue_from StandardError, with: :handle_error
def handle_error(e) render json: { error: e.message }, status: 500 end end # Bypasses centralized ExceptionHandling concern
example.good: class MyModuleController < ApplicationController def show result = MyService.call(params[:id]) render json: result # No rescue needed: ExceptionHandling handles it end end
